note: Reached by an accessible cog train up the mountain, then lifts and escalators bring visitors to the terrace near the statue; the final platform has some steps but panoramic viewing is largely accessible. Confirm the lift/escalator route.
The two-stage cable cars have step-free/accessible boarding and the station platforms and viewpoints are largely accessible; a spectacular and manageable ascent. Confirm accessible cars.
The famous wave-patterned promenade is flat, wide, paved and fully step-free the length of the beach; beach wheelchairs and mats available at points. Sand aside, easy and iconic.
The Ipanema promenade is flat and step-free with sunset views from the Arpoador rock area (the very rock is uneven but the viewpoint approach is largely level). Promenade easy; rock uneven.
The famous mosaic staircase is, by nature, a long flight of steps with no accessible route; you can view the base from the flat street but not ascend. Base viewable; steps not accessible.
The bohemian hilltop neighborhood is steep and cobbled with the historic tram and stepped lanes; charming but very hard for wheels and strollers. Some flat spots at the top reachable by car.
A large botanical garden with many flat, wide paved paths, accessible facilities and loaner wheelchairs; some gravel and gentle slopes but broadly easy and lovely.
A striking modern museum on the revitalized port with full lift access, step-free galleries and accessible ramps; the surrounding waterfront plaza is flat. Easy.
The landmark aqueduct arches are viewed from the flat, step-free plaza in Lapa; easy to reach and photograph, lively and central, busy at night.
The iconic stadium has accessible seating, ramps and lifts for events and tours; modern facilities make it broadly accessible with advance booking of accessible spots.
A scenic park mansion at the foot of Corcovado; the courtyard and cafe are largely flat and accessible but the forest trails behind are steep and unpaved. Mansion area accessible; trails not.
